posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
Yes, THAT time!

2009 Raveneau Chablis - As with the 10/11 I’ve had recently, these AOC wines are drinking wonderful! Ripe vintage orchard fruit shows some buttered popcorn…sautéed peaches, yet plenty lifted brightness of lemon sour, crushed minerality…shows perfect Chablis with its salty sea air, oyster, chalky limestone extract…and yes, classic Rav spearmint wax. You can tell the lineage as the complexity is not quite leveled as a 1er…but still, a hell of a bottle at age 17, and a true pleasure to drink! 94pts

DIAM cork…never seen one on a Raveneau?

IMG_85041920×2560 363 KB

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
2000 Noel Verset Cornas - From my last magnum, and got to share it with my brother, who barrel tasted this wine with Noel and his brother, in their “garage”! Tons of sediment…the wine is friggin amazing! Loaded with Rhône berry pie fruit…more rust cherry, med bodied and SO elegant! Amazing complexities of leather, tobacco mint leaves, black pepper, olive tapenade…bright streak of cranberry acidity lifts the wine through…just kept gaining and gaining in elegance and complexity…very bitter sweet to see this bottle go! 98pts

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
Laurent Perrier Cuvée Rose - I believe 2022 dg…charming wine with some aged good funk of Pinot skin, raspberry tarts…bright and racy blood orange tingly acids…dried roses and red licorice…very engaging dry candied fruits finish…long savory finish. Love this! 94pts

posts.name: Ramon Cabrera
posts.cooked: I had a 4-pack 2000 Verset purchased in (circa) 2010 and have opened 3 bottles over the years and, quite sadly, none was in as good a form as your bottle is. One was obviously more advanced than it should, the other 2 seem to be just not cleanly-made wines and showed the diverse wildness that Verset developed a bit of reputation with. Fingers-crossed that my last bottle in storage would be able to make up for the disappointments.

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: I’ve had a tiny number of truly ethereal Verset bottles, and probably an equal number of high VA, almost pickle-y bottles.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: 2015 for sure… and I think 2022

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
2023 Platt Vineyards Estate Chardonnay - Very surprised(well not really) at how complete and tasty this was. Full bodied, in between the the 22 Roy Piper(benchmark) and the 19 Rivers Marie in richness…wonderful mix of dried candied fruits, on the tropical side…with a huge beam of tangerine and lemon squeeze…chalky dryness, crushed rocks, pretty flower florals…in super balance right now! I really like this wine…and the reserve is better?? 96pts
